fields.name: browse
fields.description: Permission to browse or subscribe to emails

fields.name: reply
fields.description: Permission to reply to existing threads

fields.name: post
fields.description: Permission to start new threads

fields.name: moderate
fields.description: Permission to moderate the list

types.name: ActivitySubscriptionCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating subscriptions

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: ByteRange
types.description: A byte range.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Inclusive start byte offset.

fields.name: end
fields.description: Exclusive end byte offset.

types.name: ConfirmationToken
types.description: Confirmation token associated with a subscription request

fields.name: sender
fields.description:
The entity which sent this email. Will be a User if it can be associated
with an account, or a Mailbox otherwise.

fields.name: received
fields.description: Time we received this email (non-forgable).

fields.name: date
fields.description: Time given by Date header (forgable).

fields.name: subject
fields.description: The Subject header.

fields.name: messageID
fields.description: The Message-ID header, without angle brackets.

fields.name: inReplyTo
fields.description: The In-Reply-To header, if present, without angle brackets.

fields.name: header
fields.description:
Provides the value (or values) of a specific header from this email. Note
that the returned value is coerced to UTF-8 and may be lossy under certain
circumstances.

fields.name: addressList
fields.description: Retrieves the value of an address list header, such as To or Cc.

fields.name: body
fields.description: The decoded text/plain message part of the email, i.e. email body.

fields.name: rawMessage
fields.description: A URL from which the full raw message may be downloaded.

types.name: EmailCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating emails

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: GeneralACL
types.description:
An ACL entry that applies "generally", for example the rights which apply to
all subscribers to a list.

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as "4") or integer (such as 4) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: Mailbox
types.description: A mailbox not associated with a registered user

fields.name: permitMime
fields.description:
List of globs for permitted or rejected mimetypes on this list
e.g. text/*

fields.name: threads
fields.description: List of threads on this list in order of most recently bumped

fields.name: emails
fields.description: List of emails received on this list in reverse chronological order

fields.name: patches
fields.description: List of patches received on this list in order of most recently bumped

fields.name: message
fields.description: Get a specific email archived in this list by its Message-ID

fields.name: importing
fields.description: True if an import operation is underway for this list

fields.name: access
fields.description: The access that applies to this user for this list

fields.name: subscription
fields.description: The user's subscription for this list, if any

fields.name: archive
fields.description: URLs to application/mbox archives for this mailing list

fields.name: acl
fields.description: Access control list entries for this mailing list

fields.name: webhooks
fields.description:
Returns a list of mailing list webhook subscriptions. For clients
authenticated with a personal access token, this returns all webhooks
configured by all GraphQL clients for your account. For clients
authenticated with an OAuth 2.0 access token, this returns only webhooks
registered for your client.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description: Returns details of a mailing list webhook subscription by its ID.

fields.name: subscriptions
fields.description:
Get the list of subscriptions to this mailing list.

Internal use only.

fields.name: userACL
fields.description:
Get access permissions for a specific user by their email address. If there
is a related ACL entry for the given email address or an account linked to
that address, it will be returned. Otherwise, the default ACL of the mailing
list will be returned.

Internal use only.

types.name: MailingListACL
types.description:
These ACLs are configured for specific entities, and may be used to expand or
constrain the rights of a participant.

types.name: MailingListACLCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating ACL entries

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: MailingListCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating mailing lists

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: createMailingList
fields.description: Creates a new mailing list

fields.name: updateMailingList
fields.description: Updates a mailing list.

fields.name: deleteMailingList
fields.description: Deletes a mailing list

fields.name: updateUserACL
fields.description: Adds or updates the ACL for a user on a mailing list

fields.name: updateSenderACL
fields.description: Adds or updates the ACL for an email address on a mailing list

fields.name: updateMailingListACL
fields.description:
Updates the default ACL for a mailing list, which applies to users and
senders for whom a more specific ACL does not exist.

fields.name: deleteACL
fields.description:
Removes a mailing list ACL. Following this, the default mailing list ACL will
apply to this user.

fields.name: updatePatchset
fields.description: Updates the status of a patchset

fields.name: createTool
fields.description: Create a new patchset tool

fields.name: updateTool
fields.description: Updates the status of a patchset tool by its ID

fields.name: mailingListSubscribe
fields.description: Creates a mailing list subscription

fields.name: mailingListUnsubscribe
fields.description: Deletes a mailing list subscription

fields.name: importMailingListSpool
fields.description: Imports a mail spool (must be in the Mbox format)

fields.name: createUserWebhook
fields.description:
Creates a new user webhook subscription. When an event from the
provided list of events occurs, the 'query' parameter (a GraphQL query)
will be evaluated and the results will be sent to the provided URL as the
body of an HTTP POST request. The list of events must include at least one
event, and no duplicates.

This query is evaluated in the webhook context, such that query { webhook }
may be used to access details of the event which trigged the webhook. The
query may not make any mutations.

fields.name: deleteUserWebhook
fields.description:
Deletes a user webhook. Any events already queued may still be
delivered after this request completes. Clients authenticated with a
personal access token may delete any webhook registered for their account,
but authorized OAuth 2.0 clients may only delete their own webhooks.
Manually deleting a webhook configured by a third-party client may cause
unexpected behavior with the third-party integration.

fields.name: createMailingListWebhook
fields.description: Creates a new mailing list webhook.

fields.name: deleteMailingListWebhook
fields.description: Deletes a mailing list webhook.

fields.name: triggerUserEmailWebhooks
fields.description:
Triggers user webhooks for an email.

The result can be null if the user does not have browse access to the
archived email. In this case, no webhook will be triggered.

fields.name: deleteUser
fields.description: Deletes the authenticated user's account. Internal use only.

fields.name: archiveMessage
fields.description:
Archives an email (RFC 2045 MIME message) into a mailing list.
Internal use only.

fields.name: requestSubscription
fields.description:
Request a subscription to a mailing list for a given email address. If the
email is linked to a registered account, the subscription will be associated
to the account. Otherwise, it will be associated to the mailbox. If a pending
subscription request already exists for that list and email pair, the
existing confirmation token will be returned.

Internal use only.

fields.name: confirmSubscription
fields.description:
Confirm a subscription request with a token previously obtained with
requestSubscription().

Internal use only.

fields.name: requestUnsubscription
fields.description:
Request unsubscription from a mailing list for a given email address.

Internal use only.

fields.name: confirmUnsubscription
fields.description:
Confirm an unsubscription request with a token previously obtained with
requestUnsubscription().

Internal use only.

fields.name: updatePreferences
fields.description: Updates the user's preferences.

types.name: Patch
types.description:
Information parsed from a patch emailed by git send-email. Given the following
subject line:

    [PATCH myproject v2 3/4] Add foo to bar

The following fields are produced:

    index: 3
    count: 4
    version: 2
    prefix: "myproject"
    subject: "Add foo to bar"

fields.name: trailers
fields.description: git-style commit trailers parsed from the commit message of the patch.

fields.name: mbox
fields.description: URL to an application/mbox archive of only the patches in this thread

types.name: PatchsetCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating patchsets

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: PatchsetTool
types.description:
Used to add some kind of indicator for a third-party process associated with
a patchset, such as a CI service validating the change.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Returns API version information

fields.name: me
fields.description: Returns the authenticated user

fields.name: user
fields.description: Looks up a specific user

fields.name: list
fields.description: Looks up a list based on its resource ID.

fields.name: email
fields.description: Looks up a specific email by its ID

fields.name: patchset
fields.description: Looks up a patchset by ID

fields.name: subscriptions
fields.description: List of subscriptions of the authenticated user

fields.name: userWebhooks
fields.description:
Returns a list of user webhook subscriptions. For clients
authenticated with a personal access token, this returns all webhooks
configured by all GraphQL clients for your account. For clients
authenticated with an OAuth 2.0 access token, this returns only webhooks
registered for your client.

fields.name: userWebhook
fields.description: Returns details of a user webhook subscription by its ID.

fields.name: webhook
fields.description:
Returns information about the webhook currently being processed. This is
not valid during normal queries over HTTP, and will return an error if used
outside of a webhook context.

fields.name: preferences
fields.description: Returns the preferences associated with this user account.

fields.name: copySelf
fields.description:
If email corresponds to a registered user, return whether they wish to
receive their own emails; otherwise return false.

Internal use only.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String`scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

fields.name: descendants
fields.description: Replies to this thread, in chronological order

fields.name: mailto
fields.description: A mailto: URI for replying to the latest message in this thread

fields.name: mbox
fields.description: URL to an application/mbox archive of this thread

fields.name: blocks
fields.description:
Thread parsed as a tree.

The returned list is never empty. The first item is guaranteed to be the root
message. The blocks are sorted in topological order.

types.name: ThreadBlock
types.description:
A block of text in an email thread.

Blocks are parts of a message's body that aren't quotes of the parent message.
A block can be a reply to a parent block, in which case the parentStart and
parentEnd fields indicate which part of the parent message is replied to. A
block can have replies, each of which will be represented by a block in the
children field.

fields.name: key
fields.description: Unique identifier for this block.

fields.name: body
fields.description: The block's plain-text content.

fields.name: parent
fields.description: Index of the parent block (if any) in Thread.blocks.

fields.name: children
fields.description:
Replies to this block.

The list items are indexes into Thread.blocks.

fields.name: source
fields.description: The email this block comes from.

fields.name: sourceRange
fields.description: The range of this block in the source email body.

fields.name: parentRange
fields.description:
If this block is a reply to a particular chunk of the parent block, this
field indicates the range of that chunk in the parent's email body.

types.name: ThreadCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating threads

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: Time
types.description: String of the format %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ

types.name: URL
types.description:
URL from which some secondary data may be retrieved. You must provide the
same Authentication header to this address as you did to the GraphQL resolver
which provided it. The URL is not guaranteed to be consistent for an extended
length of time; applications should submit a new GraphQL query each time they
wish to access the data at the provided URL.

types.name: Upload
types.description:
A file uploaded alongside the GraphQL request. This API is compatible with the
multipart request specification for requests that include uploads:

https://github.com/jaydenseric/graphql-multipart-request-spec

types.name: User
types.description: A registered user

fields.name: deprecationDate
fields.description:
If this API version is scheduled for deprecation, this is the date on which
it will stop working; or null if this API version is not scheduled for
deprecation.

fields.name: responseBody
fields.description:
These details are provided only after a response is received from the
remote server. If a response is sent whose Content-Type is not text/*, or
cannot be decoded as UTF-8, the response body will be null. It will be
truncated after 64 KiB.

types.name: WebhookDeliveryCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of webhook deliveries

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

fields.name: client
fields.description:
If this webhook was registered by an authorized OAuth 2.0 client, this
field is non-null.

fields.name: deliveries
fields.description: All deliveries which have been sent to this webhook.

fields.name: sample
fields.description: Returns a sample payload for this subscription, for testing purposes

types.name: WebhookSubscriptionCursor
types.description:
A cursor for enumerating a list of webhook subscriptions

If there are additional results available, the cursor object may be passed
back into the same endpoint to retrieve another page. If the cursor is null,
there are no remaining results to return.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.
